mappings.rationaleAI: Comprehensive coverage of painting: elements of design and composition (B.1), iconography from religious to political (B.2), media including oil, fresco, watercolor, acrylic, encaustic (B.3), kinds of painting by subject matter including portrait, landscape, still life, allegory (B.5.b), and the history of painting spanning Western and non-Western traditions through Impressionism, Surrealism, and Abstraction (B.6).

Directly covers drawing as visual art practice: design elements and composition (A.1), media including pencils, pens, pastels, crayons (A.2), kinds of drawing by subject matter and special uses including animation, illustration, and architectural drafting (A.3), and the history of drawing from prehistoric cave markings onward (A.4).

Post-Impressionist painter working in oil with bold color and impasto brushwork (B.3), producing landscapes, portraits, still lifes, and self-portraits as representational painting (B.5.b.i), within the history of Western painting that shaped Fauvism and Expressionism (B.6.a).
Encompasses painting, drawing, printmaking, and photography as core static visual arts forms, providing the overarching framework for all four divisions of this section (A, B, C, D).

Dutch Golden Age painter producing portraits, self-portraits, biblical scenes, landscapes, and genre works as representational painting (B.5.b.i), significant in Western painting history (B.6.a), and a prolific printmaker with approximately 300 etchings contributing to the history of printmaking (C.4).

Mass reproduction of text and images from woodblock printing through movable type relates to printmaking tools and techniques (C.3), though printing extends beyond artistic printmaking to industrial reproduction.

Comprehensive coverage of photography: its nature and problems as an art (D.1), equipment and techniques from camera obscura through digital sensors (D.2), and kinds of photography by subject matter and function including photojournalism and scientific applications (D.3).
Sequential image panels with text directly correspond to caricature, cartoon, and comic strip as kinds of drawing determined by special uses (A.3.b.ii).

Ukiyo-e woodblock print artist whose Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji exemplifies relief printmaking media (C.2.a), Japanese printmaking history (C.4), and non-Western painting traditions through landscape aesthetics (B.6.b).

Sistine Chapel ceiling frescoes and Last Judgment exemplify fresco as painting medium (B.3), mural painting on fixed architectural surfaces (B.5.a.i), and a landmark in Western painting history during the High Renaissance (B.6.a).
Directly covers photographic equipment — lenses, aperture, shutter mechanisms — across daguerreotype, SLR, rangefinder, and digital sensor types (D.2).

Compositions independent of recognizable visual references, from partial abstraction (Fauvism, Cubism) to total abstraction (Constructivism, De Stijl, Abstract Expressionism), directly correspond to nonrepresentational painting (B.5.b.ii).

Truthful, non-idealized representation originating in 19th-century France with Courbet relates to representational painting including genre and narrative painting (B.5.b.i) within Western painting history (B.6.a).
The broader concept of art and aesthetics provides foundational context for drawing (A) and painting (B) as core fine arts.

Surveys aesthetic visual production encompassing the history of drawing (A.4), Western painting from Renaissance through 20th-century avant-gardes (B.6.a), non-Western painting (B.6.b), and printmaking across cultures (C.4).
Inventor of the European movable-type printing press, relevant to printmaking tools and techniques (C.3) as a milestone in mechanical reproduction.
Major artistic movement emphasizing subjectivity, imagination, and nature, with French painting (Delacroix) directly significant in the history of Western painting (B.6.a).
Foundational figure in animation (A.3.b.i), pioneering synchronized sound and full-color Technicolor animated drawing.
Recorded the earliest description of camera obscura optics (D.2), a precursor technology to photographic equipment.
Moving images captured by cameras connect to motion-picture photography as a kind of functional photography (D.3.b).
Color perception, mixing (additive and subtractive), and color spaces (RGB, CMYK) are fundamental elements of design in both drawing (A.1) and painting (B.1).
Paper as a surface for drawing media (A.2) and as the substrate for relief printmaking processes including woodcuts (C.2.a).

The Mona Lisa and The Last Supper as major works in Western painting history (B.6.a), his systematic notebooks as significant contributions to the history of drawing (A.4), and his mastery of painting media (B.3).
Lenses are foundational components in cameras for focusing light (D.2), directly relevant to photographic equipment and techniques.
